beats.activity: Wake up, coffee, check weather and bike condition for the day ahead.
beats.reasoning: Mei wakes early on a nice day to prep for work; 77°F and breezy is perfect cycling weather, and she'll want to beat any heat or crowds.

beats.activity: Bike commute through Capitol Hill to the shop.
beats.location_name: Wallingford to Downtown Seattle via Capitol Hill
beats.reasoning: Mei loves the early ride before the day heats up; Capitol Hill route is familiar and pleasant in good weather, and she gets to enjoy the Friday morning vibe.

beats.activity: Work: tune-ups, repairs, and customer consultations at the bike shop.
beats.reasoning: Friday morning is steady at the shop; Mei settles into the meditative work of fixing derailleurs and helping customers, especially those prepping for Pride weekend rides.

beats.activity: Walk to Pike Place Market for lunch.
beats.reasoning: Mei's favorite lunch spot on warm days; she'll grab fresh greens and stay hydrated before heading back to the shop.

beats.activity: Lunch: salad and water at Pike Place Market.
beats.reasoning: Mei fuels up with cold greens and hydration on a warm day; she's organized enough to eat early to avoid the Pride crowd buildup.

beats.activity: Return to shop and continue work: inventory, repairs, and final customer consultations.
beats.reasoning: Mei finishes her Friday shift with the usual mix of repairs; she stays focused on work and plans to leave before the Pride street fair traffic and road closures intensify on Broadway and Pike.

beats.activity: Bike commute home through quieter side streets to avoid Pride road closures.
beats.reasoning: Mei leaves the shop before rush hour and Pride crowds; she takes a familiar route through side streets to avoid Broadway and Pike closures, arriving home with time to freshen up.

beats.activity: Freshen up at home, change into comfortable clothes, and decide on evening plans.
beats.reasoning: Mei takes time to cool down and recharge; she's organized enough to plan her evening before the Pride fair gets too crowded, and she values personal time over big crowds.

beats.activity: Bike ride to Capitol Hill for Pride street fair.; Enjoy Pride street fair: walk around, chat with friends, grab food and drinks.
beats.location_name: Capitol Hill Street Fair (Capitol Hill, Seattle)
beats.reasoning: Mei heads out to experience the Pride kickoff; she bikes over to join the festive atmosphere, arriving early enough to enjoy the vibe before peak crowds, and she can bike home whenever she wants.

beats.activity: Bike ride home through quieter streets as the fair winds down.
beats.reasoning: Mei leaves the fair before it gets too late; she bikes home in the cool evening air, enjoying the peaceful ride back to Wallingford.

beats.activity: Wind down at home: shower, sketch kayaking routes for the weekend, organize gear.
beats.reasoning: Mei settles in for the evening with her favorite ritual—planning her weekend kayaking adventure on Lake Union; organizing and sketching makes her feel grounded and prepared.
Mei gets a solid 7.5 hours of rest after a full day of work and socializing; she'll wake refreshed for Saturday's kayaking plans.

diary:
Woke up to perfect weather—77 and breezy, exactly what you hope for on a Friday. Knocked out a handful of tune-ups at the shop this morning, nothing too gnarly. Grabbed a quick salad at Pike Place around noon; the market was already buzzing with Pride energy even though the street fair didn't kick off until evening.

Skipped the main roads home and took the quieter streets to miss the closures. Changed into something comfortable and headed back to Capitol Hill around three. The fair was packed but in that good way—colorful, loud, full of people I actually knew. Ran into Derek and his partner by the food vendors, we caught up over some street tacos and local beer. Rode home through the side streets as things wound down, still warm out.

Back home now, showered off the day, and I'm sketching kayaking routes for tomorrow's guided tour. Feeling good. Tired in that satisfied way.
thought_process: I knew the Pride closures would mess with the evening commute, so I planned the route home early—avoids stress and keeps me on the quieter streets I prefer anyway. The morning was steady work, nothing that demanded much thought. Pike Place at lunch was the right call; I needed to step outside the shop for a bit. By afternoon, I was actually looking forward to the fair. At fifty-five, I could've easily skipped it, but the weather was too good and I knew I'd see people I cared about. The vibe downtown felt celebratory without being overwhelming. I biked there instead of driving because, well, I'm a bike mechanic—feels right to show up on two wheels. Now I'm winding down thinking about tomorrow's kayak guide work, sketching routes, feeling the satisfaction of a day that balanced work, community, and a little bit of joy.
world_event_prompt: Warm, breezy Friday, high of 77F. Seattle Pride kickoff: Capitol Hill street fair and road closures along Broadway and Pike from 5pm. Festive, busy evening downtown.
